calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lon Lazar appeared by zoom for Osamah Suleiman.

The court noted that this was put on calendar because there previously was an objector. No one appeared to object. The First Amended Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission. The court informed counsel that it will modify language in the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Continued To Feb-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-28-2022 Continued To Jan-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Continued To Dec-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Osamah Suleiman, via Zoom, in propria persona, Personal Representative.

The court and Mr. Suleiman discuss why he did not appear at the last scheduled hearing. The court advises that it will not impose sanctions at this time but stresses to Mr. Suleiman the importance of managing this matter and suggests that he consider hiring an attorney. The court points Mr. Suleiman to the unauthorized distributions and of the unanswered Examiner's notes and the importance of clearing those notes by formally filing responses. After additional discussion regarding the status of this matter, the court advises Mr. Suleiman that it will be continuing this matter but that he will need to emphasize resolving the following issues: confirming and including the legal description of the Estate's Clay St. property in the proposed order; and providing notice to the CA Franchise Tax Board. The court reiterates that Mr. Suleiman should strongly consider hiring an attorney and advises that if there is no significant progress with this matter by the next hearing date, the court will consider suspending him.

The Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The Status hearing is continued to Dec-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lozada. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-21-2022 Continued To Oct-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Personal Representative Osamah Suleiman, in propria persona, to appear on Oct-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to explain the failure to appear and explain the failure to respond to the Examiner's notes. An Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not sanction the Personal Representative up to $500 for failure to appear is set for Oct-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Continued To Sep-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Continued To Aug-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status For Filing The Inventory And Appraisal And Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status For Filing The Inventory And Appraisal And Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-08-2022 Continued To Apr-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Osamah Suleiman appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court noted that this case goes back to 2012, that at the last hearing Judge Quinn spent some time explaining what needed to be done and that it appears nothing has been done. Mr. Suleiman stated that he filed two waivers of bond, that he contacted Mr. Bornstein (Referee) and that he submitted the inventory and appraisal yesterday. Mr. Suleiman also stated that he did not hire an attorney, that he hired an legal document assistant and that Mr. Bornstein was also very helpful. The court informed Mr. Suleiman that he still needs to file a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration and that he should get it filed as soon as possible. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, and status close of estate is continued to April 12, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status For Filing The Inventory And Appraisal And Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Continued To Feb-08-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status For Filing The Inventory And Appraisal And Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Continued To Jan-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status For Filing The Inventory And Appraisal And Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-12-2021 Continued To Dec-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Osamah Suleiman appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Ghoson Mukatash (Decedent's Sister) appeared in person in Propria Persona.

Ms. Suleiman stated that it has been a long haul, apologized to the court and asked for guidance on the final steps to get this wrapped up and get the property to Ms. Mukatash. Mr. Suleiman also stated that he tried to file the inventory and appraisal, but he was under the impression that they didn't have a Probate Referee. The court noted that Isodoor Bornstein is the appointed Referee and explained that the inventory and appraisal is the starting point. Mr. Suleiman stated that the spouse of the decedent re-married after one year, that he believes she is no longer entitled to anything and that he believes he can get waivers of an accounting from the beneficiary. Discussion was held regarding the probate process being complicated, technical, having a lot of requirements and the issue of hiring counsel. Mr. Suleiman stated that he will seek counsel if he feels it is needed and that he was under the impression that Ms. Mukatash was unable to assume the loan. Ms. Mukatash stated that she can assume the loan, that she has been paying the mortgage and that she is living in the property. Mr. Suleiman stated that the $25,000.00 payment to Wells Fargo was for credit cards under the decedent's name. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, and closing of the estate is continued to December 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-26-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Osamah Suleiman (Petitioner). Mariam Suleiman (Wife). Samir Mudgadash (Interpreter for Mrs. Suleiman). Tony Ahwal (Uncle/Interpreter).

The Court questioned Mrs. Suleiman if she is objecting to the granting of the petition this morning. Mrs. Suleiman responded (through Mr. Ahwal as interpreter) that she does not have any objections to the petition. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will (order signed).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
